facts.text: Peter Jackson filmed all three Lord of the Rings movies simultaneously over a 438-day shooting schedule in New Zealand.
The role of Aragorn was originally offered to Stuart Townsend, who was replaced by Viggo Mortensen just days into filming.
The Harry Potter franchise grossed over $7.7 billion worldwide across all eight films.
The Wizard of Oz (1939) was one of the first films to use Technicolor for its fantasy sequences, while the Kansas scenes were filmed in sepia.
Andy Serkis performed Gollum's movements and facial expressions using motion capture technology that was groundbreaking for its time.
Game of Thrones filmed in over 10 countries including Northern Ireland, Croatia, Iceland, Spain, and Morocco.
Frozen became the highest-grossing animated film of all time upon its release in 2013, earning over $1.28 billion worldwide.
J.R.R. Tolkien created over 15 languages for Middle-earth, several of which were used in dialogue for the Lord of the Rings films.
WETA Workshop created over 48,000 pieces of armor, 10,000 arrows, and 500 bows for the Lord of the Rings trilogy.
The Princess Bride (1987) was a box office disappointment on release but became one of the most beloved cult fantasy films through home video.
Daniel Radcliffe was cast as Harry Potter at age 11 after producers spotted him in a BBC adaptation of David Copperfield.
The NeverEnding Story's theme song by Limahl reached the top 10 in multiple countries and became an iconic 1980s anthem.
Coco (2017) became the highest-grossing film in Mexican box office history and won two Academy Awards.
Peter Jackson's WETA Digital developed the MASSIVE software to create the enormous battle sequences in the Lord of the Rings.
The Game of Thrones pilot episode was so poorly received in test screenings that it was almost entirely reshot.
Labyrinth (1986) starred David Bowie as the Goblin King and featured puppetry by Jim Henson's Creature Shop.
The Elder Wand in Harry Potter is one of three Deathly Hallows and is said to be the most powerful wand in existence.
Viggo Mortensen broke two toes when he kicked the orc helmet in The Two Towers—his scream of anguish was real.
Guillermo del Toro's Pan's Labyrinth (2006) won three Academy Awards and blended a dark fairy tale with the Spanish Civil War.
The Harry Potter films were primarily shot at Leavesden Studios near London, which is now the Warner Bros. Studio Tour.
Moana (2016) was praised for its authentic representation of Polynesian mythology and culture.
The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King won all 11 Academy Awards it was nominated for, tying the record with Ben-Hur and Titanic.
The original Dark Crystal (1982) used no human actors—every character was performed by puppeteers from Jim Henson's team.
George R.R. Martin began writing A Game of Thrones in 1991 and published it in 1996.
The Wizarding World of Harry Potter at Universal Studios opened in 2010 and has since expanded to parks in Hollywood, Japan, and Beijing.
Tangled (2010) had a production budget of approximately $260 million, making it one of the most expensive animated films ever made.
Sean Astin kept the pair of prosthetic hobbit feet he wore as Samwise Gamgee throughout the Lord of the Rings trilogy.
The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe (2005) grossed over $745 million worldwide.
Terry Gilliam's Brazil (1985) is considered a masterpiece of dystopian fantasy filmmaking.
The Harry Potter series used over 250 sets built at Leavesden Studios during its decade-long production.
Brave (2012) was Pixar's first film with a female protagonist and won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature.
The Lord of the Rings trilogy was filmed entirely in New Zealand, which saw a massive tourism boost as fans visited filming locations.
Stardust (2007), based on Neil Gaiman's novel, featured an ensemble cast including Robert De Niro, Claire Danes, and Michelle Pfeiffer.
The One Ring prop used in the Lord of the Rings films was created in multiple sizes for different shots and actors.
Maleficent (2014) reimagined the Sleeping Beauty villain's backstory and earned over $758 million worldwide.
Tim Burton's Edward Scissorhands (1990) was inspired by a drawing he made as a teenager of a thin, isolated figure with scissors for hands.
The Harry Potter franchise created the term 'Muggle' which was added to the Oxford English Dictionary.
Willow (1988) was directed by Ron Howard and produced by George Lucas, featuring groundbreaking morphing visual effects.
House of the Dragon premiered on HBO in August 2022 and became the network's most-watched series premiere.
The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring premiered in London on December 10, 2001.
Percy Jackson & the Olympians debuted as a Disney+ series in 2023 after two earlier film adaptations.
Ian McKellen almost turned down the role of Gandalf because he was unsure about committing to such a long shoot in New Zealand.
The Hobbit trilogy used 48 frames per second filming, double the standard 24fps, which divided audiences and critics.
Enchanted (2007) mixed live-action and animation as a Disney princess found herself in modern-day New York City.
The Conan the Barbarian (1982) role launched Arnold Schwarzenegger's film career into mainstream Hollywood.
Ursula K. Le Guin's Earthsea series has been adapted twice—as a Sci Fi Channel miniseries in 2004 and a Studio Ghibli film in 2006.
WETA Workshop built a full-size Shelob puppet that was 18 feet wide for The Return of the King.
The Game of Thrones Iron Throne on set was made from about 200 swords and took two months to construct.
Frozen 2 (2019) grossed over $1.45 billion worldwide, becoming the highest-grossing animated film at the time.
The Lord of the Rings trilogy collectively grossed nearly $3 billion at the worldwide box office.
J.K. Rowling was rejected by 12 publishers before Bloomsbury accepted Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone in 1997.
Legend (1985) starred Tom Cruise and was directed by Ridley Scott, featuring elaborate makeup by Rob Bottin.
The Rings of Power series on Amazon Prime had a reported budget of over $700 million for its first season, making it one of the most expensive TV shows ever.
C.S. Lewis and J.R.R. Tolkien were close friends and members of the Inklings literary group at Oxford.
The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers featured the Battle of Helm's Deep, which took four months of night shooting.
Guillermo del Toro spent over two years developing The Hobbit before leaving the project, which Peter Jackson then directed.
The Harry Potter theme composed by John Williams is one of the most recognizable film scores in cinema history.
Game of Thrones' final season drew an average of 44.2 million viewers per episode across all platforms.
The Dark Crystal: Age of Resistance (2019) used entirely practical puppets and won an Emmy for Outstanding Children's Program.
Excalibur (1981) directed by John Boorman was one of the definitive Arthurian legend films of the 20th century.
Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows was split into two films, a strategy later adopted by other franchises like Twilight and The Hunger Games.
The Wheel of Time series on Amazon Prime is based on Robert Jordan's 14-book epic fantasy series.
Orlando Bloom performed many of his own stunts as Legolas, including the famous shield-surfing scene in The Two Towers.
Cate Blanchett's portrayal of Galadriel in Lord of the Rings required her to learn Elvish dialogue created by linguist David Salo.
The NeverEnding Story (1984) was based on the novel by German author Michael Ende, who disliked the film adaptation.
The Fantastic Beasts franchise expanded the Wizarding World to 1920s New York and Europe.
Weta Workshop's Richard Taylor won four Academy Awards for his work on the Lord of the Rings trilogy.
Moana's ocean was animated using a proprietary system called Splash that could simulate complex water behavior.
Game of Thrones spent approximately $15 million per episode in its final two seasons.
The Harry Potter Sorting Hat was an animatronic puppet voiced by Leslie Phillips in the films.
Peter Jackson used forced perspective and oversized sets rather than CGI to make hobbits appear small next to humans.
The Eragon film (2006) was critically panned despite the popularity of Christopher Paolini's novel.
Tim Burton's Alice in Wonderland (2010) earned over $1 billion worldwide, becoming the fifth film to reach that milestone.
Valyrian steel in Game of Thrones was inspired by real-world Damascus steel, known for its distinctive wavy patterns.
Robbie Coltrane was J.K. Rowling's first choice for the role of Hagrid in the Harry Potter films.
The Lord of the Rings trilogy's extended editions add over two hours of additional footage across the three films.
Frozen's 'Let It Go' won the Academy Award for Best Original Song and became a global phenomenon.
The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey premiered in Wellington, New Zealand, on November 28, 2012.
Pan's Labyrinth features a mix of CGI and practical creature effects, with Doug Jones performing both the Faun and the Pale Man.
Game of Thrones won 59 Primetime Emmy Awards, the most of any drama series in television history.
The original Wizard of Oz Technicolor process required the set to be lit three times brighter than normal.
The Harry Potter studio tour at Leavesden attracts over 6,000 visitors per day.
Narnia's Aslan was voiced by Liam Neeson and brought to life using a combination of CGI and motion capture.
Terry Gilliam's Time Bandits (1981) mixed fantasy and comedy with a cast including Sean Connery and John Cleese.
The prop department for Lord of the Rings created over 19,000 costumes for the trilogy.
George R.R. Martin wrote several Game of Thrones episodes himself, including 'Blackwater' and 'The Lion and the Rose'.
Brave's Scottish setting was inspired by research trips to the Scottish Highlands taken by the Pixar creative team.
The Lord of the Rings Shire set in Matamata, New Zealand, was rebuilt as a permanent tourist attraction after The Hobbit filming.
Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them (2016) marked the screenwriting debut of J.K. Rowling.
The Princess Bride features the iconic line 'As you wish,' which was also the title of Cary Elwes's behind-the-scenes memoir.
Gollum's 'My Precious' was ranked among the most iconic movie lines in cinema history.
Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone was released in the US as Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone after the studio changed the title.
The Hobbit's Smaug was voiced and motion-captured by Benedict Cumberbatch, who crawled on the floor to simulate dragon movements.
Disney's Frozen was loosely based on Hans Christian Andersen's fairy tale The Snow Queen.
The Lord of the Rings trilogy was nominated for a total of 30 Academy Awards across all three films.
House of the Dragon is set approximately 200 years before the events of Game of Thrones.
Guillermo del Toro directed Hellboy (2004) and its sequel, bringing Mike Mignola's dark fantasy comic to life.
The Witcher series on Netflix debuted in 2019, starring Henry Cavill as Geralt of Rivia.
The prosthetic ears worn by the elf actors in Lord of the Rings were individually molded for each performer.
Coco won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature and Best Original Song for 'Remember Me.'
The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe's battle sequences were filmed in New Zealand, similar to Lord of the Rings.
The Princess Bride was written by William Goldman, who also wrote the screenplays for Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid and All the President's Men.
Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire was the first film in the series to receive a PG-13 rating.
Peter Jackson's King Kong (2005) featured groundbreaking motion capture work with Andy Serkis that built on the Gollum technology.
Game of Thrones costume designer Michele Clapton won three Emmy Awards for her work on the series.
The Wizard of Oz's ruby slippers are among the most valuable pieces of movie memorabilia, with a surviving pair valued at over $2 million.
Tangled features 140,000 individually animated strands of hair on Rapunzel's head.
The Lord of the Rings used miniatures so detailed they were called 'bigatures,' some measuring up to 30 feet wide.
The Harry Potter series was translated into over 80 languages, making it the most translated book series in history.
Maleficent: Mistress of Evil (2019) expanded Angelina Jolie's titular role and explored the origins of fairy-kind.
Filming for The Hobbit trilogy lasted 266 days across New Zealand.
Game of Thrones' Battle of the Bastards episode took 25 days to film and involved 500 extras and 70 horses.
The Dark Crystal (1982) was directed by Jim Henson and Frank Oz and took five years to produce.
Alan Rickman was J.K. Rowling's personal choice for Severus Snape, and she shared key plot secrets with him before anyone else.
Tim Burton's Big Fish (2003) blended reality and fantasy in a story about a son trying to understand his father's tall tales.
The Wheel of Time's first season premiered on Amazon Prime Video on November 19, 2021.
WETA Digital created over 1,500 visual effects shots for The Return of the King alone.
Frozen's Elsa was originally written as a villain but was reimagined after the song 'Let It Go' was composed.
The Golden Compass (2007) won the Academy Award for Best Visual Effects despite underperforming at the US box office.
Viggo Mortensen learned to sword-fight for Lord of the Rings and often carried his prop sword with him off-set.
Percy Jackson author Rick Riordan was heavily involved in the Disney+ adaptation after being unhappy with the earlier Fox films.
Harry Potter's Diagon Alley set at Leavesden Studios contained over 20,000 individually crafted prop items.
Stardust (2007) was based on Neil Gaiman's illustrated novel and directed by Matthew Vaughn.
The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King remains the only fantasy film to win the Academy Award for Best Picture.
Game of Thrones was filmed at Dubrovnik's city walls, which doubled as King's Landing, creating a major tourism draw for Croatia.
Moana directors Ron Clements and John Musker formed an 'Oceanic Story Trust' of Pacific Island cultural advisors.
The NeverEnding Story's animatronic Falkor the Luckdragon was 43 feet long and operated by multiple puppeteers.
Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix was the longest book in the series but became one of the shortest films.
The Chronicles of Narnia: Prince Caspian (2008) struggled at the box office, earning less than its predecessor.
Peter Jackson was originally approached to direct a single Lord of the Rings film before convincing the studio to make three.
Conan the Barbarian author Robert E. Howard created the character in 1932 for the pulp magazine Weird Tales.
Enchanted's Amy Adams performed her own singing in the film and received widespread praise for her comedic timing.
The Harry Potter films featured over 600 child extras throughout the series.
Labyrinth's ballroom scene used an innovative combination of practical effects, puppetry, and in-camera tricks.
Rings of Power featured a cast of relative newcomers alongside established actors like Morfydd Clark and Robert Aramayo.
The Lord of the Rings trilogy's Howard Shore score was performed by the London Philharmonic Orchestra.
Game of Thrones' Red Wedding episode 'The Rains of Castamere' is widely regarded as one of the most shocking TV moments ever.
Guillermo del Toro's The Shape of Water (2017) won the Academy Award for Best Picture, blending fantasy and romance.
The Hobbit trilogy collectively grossed over $2.9 billion at the worldwide box office.
Frozen cosplay became one of the most popular costume choices at conventions after the film's release.
The Lord of the Rings Extended Edition of The Return of the King runs 4 hours and 11 minutes.
Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban was directed by Alfonso Cuarón, who brought a darker visual style to the series.
The Wizard of Oz originally flopped at the box office and only became profitable through re-releases and television broadcasts.
Willow's morphing sequence where Fin Raziel transforms through multiple animals was a pioneering achievement in digital effects.
Game of Thrones' composer Ramin Djawadi also composed the iconic Westworld theme.
Brave's protagonist Merida was added to the official Disney Princess lineup in 2013.
The One Ring inscription in Black Speech was invented by Tolkien and reads 'One Ring to rule them all, One Ring to find them.'
WETA Workshop created a full-size cave troll puppet for The Fellowship of the Ring that weighed over 250 pounds.
The Harry Potter and the Cursed Child stage play opened in London's West End in 2016.
House of the Dragon's first-season finale drew nearly 10 million viewers on its premiere night.
The Narnia franchise was produced at various locations including New Zealand, the Czech Republic, and Australia.
Pan's Labyrinth's Pale Man creature was designed to evoke images of child-eating monsters from European folklore.
Terry Gilliam's The Adventures of Baron Munchausen (1988) was a notorious box office disaster despite critical acclaim.
The Lord of the Rings' Rivendell set was built in Kaitoke Regional Park near Wellington, New Zealand.
Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince earned $394 million worldwide in its first five days.
Disney's live-action Beauty and the Beast (2017) grossed over $1.26 billion worldwide.
Game of Thrones pioneered the use of large-scale battle sequences in television, rivaling big-budget films.
Cate Blanchett learned to shoot a bow for her role as Galadriel in The Hobbit trilogy.
The Princess Bride's Inigo Montoya, played by Mandy Patinkin, delivered one of the most-quoted lines in film history.
Coco's Land of the Dead was inspired by the Mexican holiday Día de los Muertos and featured over a million animated buildings.
The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug introduced the character Tauriel, played by Evangeline Lilly, who was created for the films.
Sean Bean's death scene as Boromir in The Fellowship of the Ring has become iconic in cinema.
J.R.R. Tolkien served in World War I, and his experiences in the trenches influenced the Dead Marshes and the Battle of the Pelennor Fields.
The Wheel of Time features a magic system called 'channeling' that draws on a source called the One Power.
Frozen was the first Disney animated film to be directed by a woman—Jennifer Lee co-directed with Chris Buck.
Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows – Part 2 is the highest-grossing Harry Potter film, earning over $1.34 billion.
The Lord of the Rings' Mines of Moria sequence was inspired by real abandoned mines in New Zealand.
Guillermo del Toro created a detailed notebook filled with drawings and notes that guided the design of Pan's Labyrinth.
Tim Burton's Corpse Bride (2005) was made using stop-motion animation with puppets that had mechanical faces.
Game of Thrones' Dothraki and Valyrian languages were created by linguist David J. Peterson.
The Dark Crystal: Age of Resistance used 3D printing to create some of its puppet characters.
Maleficent was the fourth highest-grossing film of 2014 worldwide.
The Lord of the Rings Shire scenes were filmed in Matamata, where 37 hobbit holes were constructed.
Ridley Scott's Legend (1985) featured extensive makeup by Rob Bottin, who also designed The Thing's creature effects.
Harry Potter's Quidditch matches were created using a combination of wire work, green screen, and CGI.
Tangled's floating lantern scene required animators to render over 45,000 individually lit lanterns.
Game of Thrones' first episode, 'Winter Is Coming,' premiered on HBO on April 17, 2011.
The NeverEnding Story II: The Next Chapter (1990) was a sequel that performed poorly compared to the original.
Elijah Wood auditioned for the role of Frodo by filming himself in a homemade hobbit costume in the woods.
The Percy Jackson Disney+ series filmed in Vancouver, British Columbia, standing in for Camp Half-Blood.
WETA Workshop created prosthetic hobbit feet from silicone that took 90 minutes to apply each morning.
Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets featured a 60-foot animatronic basilisk built by the creature effects team.
The Hobbit films used 3D cameras developed by James Cameron's company for the production.
Ursula K. Le Guin publicly criticized the 2004 Earthsea adaptation for whitewashing her characters.
Game of Thrones' opening credits sequence was created by Elastic and won multiple Emmy Awards.
Peter Jackson lost over 70 pounds before filming The Lord of the Rings and kept the weight off throughout production.
Stardust featured Robert De Niro in an unusual comedic role as a sky pirate captain.
The Wizard of Oz's tornado sequence was created using a muslin stocking spun with a motor and dust.
Harry Potter filming at Christ Church, Oxford provided the inspiration for the Hogwarts Great Hall set.
Frozen 2 explored the origins of Elsa's powers and introduced the enchanted forest of Northuldra.
The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring earned over $871 million worldwide.
George R.R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire series has sold over 90 million copies worldwide.
Labyrinth's 'Dance Magic Dance' sequence remains one of the most beloved musical numbers in fantasy film.
The Witcher's Henry Cavill was such a fan of the books and games that he actively lobbied for the role of Geralt.
Brave's archery sequences were animated with reference to professional archer consultants.
Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone premiered in London on November 4, 2001.
The Lord of the Rings' Helm's Deep battle used over 20,000 computer-generated soldiers via the MASSIVE software.
Enchanted was originally conceived in the 1990s but spent over a decade in development at Disney.
The Rings of Power recreated a younger version of Middle-earth set during the Second Age of Tolkien's mythology.
Game of Thrones' use of direwolves required a mix of real Northern Inuit dogs and CGI enhancement.
Conan the Barbarian (1982) was filmed primarily in Spain and featured a score by Basil Poledouris.
Moana's demigod Maui was voiced by Dwayne Johnson, who also performed the song 'You're Welcome.'
The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies was dedicated to the memory of actor Ian Holm, though he appeared via flashback.
Tim Burton's Sleepy Hollow (1999) was a gothic fantasy reimagining of Washington Irving's classic tale.
The Harry Potter Yule Ball scene in Goblet of Fire required 300 extras and took three weeks to film.
Peter Jackson initially envisioned The Lord of the Rings as two films before New Line Cinema agreed to fund three.
C.S. Lewis's The Chronicles of Narnia has sold over 100 million copies in 47 languages worldwide.
The Lord of the Rings trilogy required over 20,000 extras throughout its production.
The Game of Thrones finale attracted 19.3 million viewers, making it the most-watched HBO episode at the time.
Frozen merchandise generated over $5.3 billion in retail sales within its first year of release.
Guillermo del Toro's Crimson Peak (2015) was a gothic romance with elaborate practical sets and creature effects.
Harry Potter's Dobby was the first fully CGI character in the series and required 800 render hours per frame.
The Princess Bride's sword fight between Westley and Inigo Montoya was choreographed over two weeks by fight master Bob Anderson.
Willow was nominated for two Academy Awards for Best Visual Effects and Best Sound Editing.
Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader (2010) was the last film in the franchise to date.
The Lord of the Rings cast got matching Elvish tattoos reading 'nine' to honor the Fellowship.
Game of Thrones used real wolves from a sanctuary in Northern Ireland during early seasons.
Coco director Lee Unkrich spent six years researching Mexican culture and traditions before making the film.
The Dark Crystal featured 'Gelfling' characters that were among the most complex puppets ever created by the Henson workshop.
Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban introduced the Time-Turner, one of the series' most iconic magical artifacts.
The Rings of Power premiered on Amazon Prime Video on September 2, 2022, with its first two episodes.
Tangled's animation team studied the paintings of French Rococo artist Jean-Honoré Fragonard for the film's visual style.
The Lord of the Rings' Gandalf the White reveal scene took multiple takes as Ian McKellen kept laughing.
House of the Dragon's dragon visual effects were handled by several VFX studios, including Industrial Light & Magic.
The Wizard of Oz's original Tin Man, Buddy Ebsen, was hospitalized after an allergic reaction to the aluminum makeup.
Terry Gilliam was originally set to direct the Harry Potter films before Chris Columbus was chosen.
The Game of Thrones costumes were so detailed that each contained hidden symbols relevant to the character's storyline.
Brave was originally titled 'The Bear and the Bow' during its early development at Pixar.
Fantasy film merchandise and tie-in products generate billions of dollars annually across all major franchises.
The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers premiered on December 18, 2002.
Eragon (2006) opened at number one at the US box office but received mostly negative reviews.
The Harry Potter series employed over 4,000 crew members across its entire production.
Pan's Labyrinth's Faun costume took five hours to apply and required Doug Jones to perform on stilts.
The Lord of the Rings' Barad-dûr tower miniature stood over 9 feet tall and was highly detailed.
Game of Thrones' Night King was played by two different actors across the series.
Disney's Wish (2023) was created to celebrate the studio's 100th anniversary and featured a fantasy kingdom.
Viggo Mortensen continued acting in a scene after chipping a tooth, and Peter Jackson kept the take in the film.
Gollum's split personality scenes were achieved by Andy Serkis performing both sides on set with other actors watching.
The NeverEnding Story's Swamps of Sadness scene where Artax the horse sinks was achieved using a mechanical platform.
The Harry Potter Universal theme park lets visitors buy interactive wands that trigger special effects throughout the park.
Frozen's Olaf was voiced by Josh Gad, who ad-libbed many of the snowman's funniest lines.
The Lord of the Rings used approximately 1,800 pairs of hobbit feet prosthetics during filming.
Game of Thrones' Cersei Lannister's Walk of Shame used a body double and visual effects compositing.
Labyrinth was a commercial disappointment in 1986 but gained a devoted cult following through home video.
Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix featured the largest set ever built for the franchise—the Hall of Prophecy.
The Princess Bride's 'inconceivable' catchphrase by Wallace Shawn became a widely quoted pop culture reference.
Peter Jackson's first film, Bad Taste (1987), was a low-budget sci-fi horror that he made over four years on weekends.
The Hobbit trilogy's 48fps format required theaters to install new projection equipment to screen the films.
Game of Thrones' Battle of the Blackwater was the first major battle sequence in the series and expanded the show's scope.
Moana's soundtrack was co-written by Lin-Manuel Miranda, who composed 'How Far I'll Go.'
The Lord of the Rings' Council of Elrond scene took over two weeks to film due to its complexity and number of characters.
Fantastic Beasts: The Crimes of Grindelwald (2018) was the lowest-grossing Wizarding World film.
WETA Digital created the first fully digital face replacement for a speaking character in a major film with Gollum.
The Wizard of Oz's 'Over the Rainbow' was almost cut from the film by MGM executives who thought it slowed the pace.
Tim Burton and Johnny Depp have collaborated on numerous fantasy films including Sweeney Todd, Charlie and the Chocolate Factory, and Dark Shadows.
The Harry Potter films used real owls for the mail delivery scenes, requiring an on-set animal trainer.
Game of Thrones locations in Dubrovnik now feature dedicated walking tours for fans.
The Lord of the Rings' Mount Doom scenes were filmed on the slopes of Mount Ruapehu, an active volcano in New Zealand.
Frozen's animation required simulating over 2,000 individual snowflake shapes.
Maleficent provided a sympathetic reinterpretation of one of Disney's most iconic villains.
Terry Gilliam's The Fisher King (1991) blended Arthurian quest mythology with a modern New York City setting.
George R.R. Martin initially envisioned A Song of Ice and Fire as a trilogy before expanding to seven planned volumes.
The Lord of the Rings Shire set featured 5,000 cubic meters of vegetation planted a year before filming began.
Harry Potter's Platform 9¾ at King's Cross Station in London became an official tourist attraction with a dedicated shop.
The Wheel of Time season one featured eight episodes and was renewed before the first season premiered.
The Dark Crystal's world of Thra was created with over 600 drawings and paintings by fantasy artist Brian Froud.
Tangled was the 50th Disney animated feature film.
The Lord of the Rings' Isengard flooding scene used 70,000 gallons of water released into a scale model set.
The Harry Potter films featured seven different directors across the eight movies.
Game of Thrones' Dragonstone was filmed at the striking Basque coastline of San Juan de Gaztelugatxe in Spain.
The Princess Bride director Rob Reiner wanted to make the film for years before finding financing.
Brave received the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature Film in 2013.
The Lord of the Rings' Balrog was created as a combination of practical fire effects and CGI by WETA Digital.
J.K. Rowling wrote the first Harry Potter book in Edinburgh cafés while living on welfare.
Coco's guitar was modeled on a real instrument style traditional to Mexican folk music.
Peter Jackson's Heavenly Creatures (1994) starred a young Kate Winslet and blended reality with fantasy sequences.
Game of Thrones' three original dragon names—Drogon, Rhaegal, and Viserion—honored fallen characters in the story.
The Lord of the Rings' Dead Marshes sequence was filmed in a flooded parking lot with underwater lighting.
Enchanted 2: Disenchanted premiered on Disney+ in 2022, 15 years after the original film.
The Hobbit's Stone Trolls scene featured giant animatronic trolls before being enhanced with CGI.
Harry Potter fans formed a massive online fandom that includes fan fiction, wizard rock bands, and academic conferences.
The NeverEnding Story's Ivory Tower was a large miniature set piece that stood over 15 feet tall during filming.
The Wizard of Oz used over 100 little people as Munchkins, the largest gathering of little people in Hollywood history at the time.
Game of Thrones' White Walker makeup won multiple Emmy Awards for Outstanding Prosthetic Makeup.
Frozen 2's 'Into the Unknown' was performed by Idina Menzel and featured in the film's climactic identity sequence.
Guillermo del Toro owns a house called 'Bleak House' filled with memorabilia, props, and original art from his films.
The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King had eleven separate endings in its theatrical cut.
J.R.R. Tolkien sold the film rights to The Lord of the Rings and The Hobbit in 1969 for just £100,000.
Harry Potter's Marauder's Map prop was so detailed it contained real passages from the books in tiny handwriting.
Game of Thrones' Hardhome battle was originally not in the books and was created specifically for the TV show.
The Rings of Power's production involved building massive practical sets in New Zealand, continuing the Tolkien filming tradition.
Labyrinth's Escher-inspired staircase room was built as a practical set with gravity-defying camera angles.
Tim Burton's Miss Peregrine's Home for Peculiar Children (2016) adapted Ransom Riggs' fantasy novel.
The Lord of the Rings' Rohirrim charge at the Battle of Pelennor Fields used 250 real horses and riders.
The Harry Potter franchise has generated over $25 billion in total revenue including films, merchandise, and theme parks.
Moana was the first Disney princess to not have a love interest in her story.
Peter Jackson's Dead Alive (1992) is considered one of the goriest films ever made, a far cry from his later fantasy epics.
Game of Thrones' Winterfell set in Northern Ireland's Castle Ward attracted over 120,000 visitors after the show aired.
The Princess Bride's 'Cliffs of Insanity' were actually the Cliffs of Moher in Ireland.
Harry Potter's Hogwarts Express scenes were filmed on the real Jacobite steam train in Scotland.
Fantasy film conventions like Dragon Con regularly attract over 80,000 attendees.
Frozen's 'For the First Time in Forever' was written by Kristen Anderson-Lopez and Robert Lopez.
WETA Workshop spent over a year designing the Ringwraith costumes and props for The Lord of the Rings.
The Harry Potter series employed a full-time on-set teacher to ensure child actors kept up with their education.
House of the Dragon renewed for a second season within a week of its premiere due to strong viewership.
The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King remains the highest-grossing film in the trilogy with $1.14 billion worldwide.
Game of Thrones composer Ramin Djawadi wrote the 'Light of the Seven' piano piece for the Season 6 finale.
Willow star Warwick Davis was only 17 years old when he was cast in the lead role.
The Wizard of Oz's horse Emerald City sequence used different colored horses coated with flavored gelatin.
The Lord of the Rings' Minas Tirith miniature was one of the largest ever built for a film, covering over 6,000 square feet.
Fantastic Beasts: The Secrets of Dumbledore was released in April 2022.
Terry Gilliam's Monty Python and the Holy Grail (1975) is a beloved comedic take on Arthurian fantasy.
The Harry Potter series features 772 spells that were compiled and organized by the prop and script departments.
Game of Thrones' makeup department applied over 3,000 prosthetic pieces throughout the final season.
Brave's animated bears required Pixar to develop new software for realistic fur simulation.

Coco's bridge of marigold petals required millions of individually placed digital flower particles.
The Dark Crystal director Jim Henson considered it his masterwork and was deeply disappointed by its initial reception.

Peter Jackson screened early footage from The Fellowship of the Ring at the 2001 Cannes Film Festival to overwhelming response.

Tangled's Mother Gothel was designed to be an older, manipulative contrast to Rapunzel's youthful optimism.

The Princess Bride was released on September 25, 1987, and earned $30.9 million domestically.

Guillermo del Toro's notebook for Pan's Labyrinth was published as a standalone art book.

Stardust features a memorable duel scene choreographed on a cloud between Charlie Cox and Robert De Niro.

The Game of Thrones prequel House of the Dragon premiered on August 21, 2022.

The Wizard of Oz's Glinda originally had a much larger role in early scripts before being reduced.
The Harry Potter films employed every major special effects technique: practical creatures, CGI, wirework, animatronics, and motion capture.

The Lord of the Rings' Sean Bean was so afraid of helicopter travel that he hiked to mountain filming locations in full Boromir costume.
Frozen's Arendelle was inspired by the real Norwegian town of Bergen and its colorful wooden houses.
Game of Thrones spent over $30 million on dragon visual effects throughout the series.
The NeverEnding Story III (1994) is widely regarded as the weakest entry in the franchise.
Tim Burton's Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (2005) reimagined Roald Dahl's classic with a darker fantasy tone.
The Harry Potter books have sold over 500 million copies worldwide, making them the best-selling book series in history.

WETA Workshop created a full-scale dead Oliphaunt prop for The Return of the King weighing several tons.
Game of Thrones' pilot was directed by Tom McCarthy, who later won an Academy Award for Spotlight.
The Lord of the Rings trilogy was shot using over six million feet of film stock.

Terry Gilliam's Twelve Monkeys (1995) blended science fiction and fantasy themes in a time-travel narrative.
